neighborhoods.name: The Fan
neighborhoods.description: Richmond's signature neighborhood of colorful Victorian rowhouses on tree-lined streets that fan out from Monroe Park
neighborhoods.character: Urban walkable, eclectic, vibrant, architecturally rich

housing.predominantType: Victorian and Edwardian rowhouses, many divided into apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Strong restaurant and cafe scene along Robinson and Lombardy Streets. VCU campus borders the area. Monument Avenue runs through the northern edge.

neighborhoods.name: Carytown
neighborhoods.description: Walkable shopping and dining district along Cary Street with independent boutiques, restaurants, and the historic Byrd Theatre

housing.predominantType: Apartments above shops, bungalows, and small houses on side streets

neighborhoods.character_notes: Annual Watermelon Festival draws large crowds. Byrd Theatre shows second-run films for a few dollars. Excellent walkability to daily needs.

neighborhoods.name: Church Hill
neighborhoods.description: Richmond's oldest neighborhood on a hilltop east of downtown, centered on St. John's Church where Patrick Henry gave his famous speech
neighborhoods.character: Historic, revitalizing, community-focused, views of the James River

housing.predominantType: Federal and Greek Revival row houses, many recently renovated

neighborhoods.character_notes: Rapidly gentrifying with new restaurants and cafes. Chimborazo Park offers panoramic city views. Some blocks are very polished, others still transitioning.

neighborhoods.name: Short Pump
neighborhoods.description: Affluent western suburb in Henrico County with upscale shopping, top-rated schools, and modern residential developments

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes in planned communities and luxury townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Short Pump Town Center is a major retail destination. Excellent healthcare access -- Henrico Doctors Hospital nearby. 20 minutes to downtown Richmond.

neighborhoods.name: Museum District
neighborhoods.description: Refined residential neighborhood anchored by the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts, with stately homes and quiet, leafy streets

housing.predominantType: Tudor and Colonial Revival houses, apartments in converted homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: VMFA is free and world-class. Boulevard is a major corridor with dining options. Quieter and slightly more upscale than The Fan next door.
